methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Nu Quantum is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 quantum computing firms: three public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and eight private-stage competitors including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the quantum computing sector—only five peers exist in the narrow sector, but none met the threshold of three or more filtered datapoints at matching valuation tier or stage. Accordingly, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-later-stage advancement. Using the $60M total raised as a valuation proxy, this method yields $120M.

The triangulated valuation range is $102M (low) to $138M (high), with a median of $120M. This reflects a single applicable method and underscores a key uncertainty: the absence of sufficient same-stage quantum computing comparables limits conviction in the median estimate. Refinement would require either additional Series A quantum peers with disclosed terms or alternative approaches anchored to enterprise metrics or revenue multiples—data not available in the current set. Users should treat this range as preliminary pending access to narrower peer data or operational performance benchmarks.
